title: FieldTrace-OCR: A Reliability-Oriented Evaluation Protocol for OCR-Based Invoice Parsing Pipelines
author: Khadka, Shaurav
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Invoice parsing is often evaluated as an optical character recognition problem even though operational failures can occur across the entire pipeline: ingestion, OCR, structured extraction, field mapping, normalisation, validation, and review. This technical note proposes FieldTrace-OCR, an individual-authored reliability evaluation protocol for OCR-based invoice parsing systems. The protocol records a field-level trace object for every extracted value and evaluates six evidence dimensions: source provenance, raw extraction, normalisation rule, confidence value, validation outcome, and escalation decision. It also defines a compact error taxonomy and a minimum release checklist. A synthetic invoice example demonstrates how an apparently plausible extraction can still require review after transformation or validation. The note does not disclose employer data, client documents, proprietary thresholds, or production metrics. FieldTrace-OCR is presented as a proposed technical protocol rather than an externally validated standard. Its purpose is to make document-intelligence claims easier to audit and harder to overstate.

keywords: document intelligence; OCR; invoice parsing; structured extraction; traceability; validation; confidence; human review; AI reliability
